title: lahaya tenuifolia
intro: Lahaya tenuifolia, also known as the "Malabar neem," is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Asia, particularly in the Philippines. This plant has been utilized for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Lahaya tenuifolia include its ability to treat various skin issues, such as acne, eczema, and wounds, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and exhibit anti-cancer properties. Lahaya tenuifolia is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
